title: Subagents, Parallelism, and Coordination · Spiral Review
summary: Coordinate specialist agents without duplicate or conflicting work. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a bounded multi-agent execution map after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Delegate only concrete independent work with a clear return contract.
Give a subagent the minimum context required without losing governing constraints.
Coordinate shared files, locks, messages, and ownership boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Bounded agent-loop portfolio. No external effects occur in this browser lab."""

stimulus.title: Coordination Cost case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Coordination Cost applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare parallel speedup with communication, merge, and review cost.
